claim: Gemini 2.0's video analyzer uses function calling alongside natural language prompting to structure output (captions, key moments, tables, counts) from video frames
actionable: Define explicit function signatures for video analysis tasks to get structured JSON output instead of unstructured text

claim: Videos longer than a few minutes can be challenging for AI Studio's video analyzer UI; Python SDK may handle longer videos more reliably
actionable: For long videos (>5 minutes), use the Python SDK directly instead of AI Studio's web interface

claim: You can customize video analysis by modifying prompts to count specific objects (trees, phones, people) frame by frame and generate structured time-coded data
actionable: Adapt the setTimeCodesWithNumericValues function prompt to count any entity in your use case

claim: Separating visual descriptions from transcripts (dual output) enables better RAG indexing by capturing both visual and spoken context independently
actionable: When indexing videos for RAG, create separate chunks for visual descriptions and transcripts to improve retrieval quality

claim: Gemini 2.0's unified SDK simplifies video processing by handling file uploads with state tracking (processing → active) before inference
actionable: Check upload state and wait for processing before calling Gemini inference on uploaded videos
